     The Numbers Business Report

Alexander and the Terrible, Horrible, No Good, Very Bad Day (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $66,954,149Details
International Box Office $34,425,138
Worldwide Box Office $101,379,287
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $11,879,632
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,674,840
Total $15,554,472
Further financial details...

Synopsis

11-year-old Alexander experiences the most terrible and horrible day of his young life—a day that begins with gum stuck in his hair, followed by one calamity after another. But when Alexander tells his upbeat family about the misadventures of his disastrous day, he finds little sympathy and begins to wonder if bad things only happen to him. He soon learns that he’s not alone when his mom, dad, brother and sister all find themselves living through their own terrible, horrible, no good, very bad day. Anyone who says there is no such thing as a bad day just hasn’t had one.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$18,360,230 (27.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.65 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:66.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$28,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,088 opening theaters/3,176 max. theaters, 6.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $97,958,906

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 10th, 2014 (Wide) by Walt Disney
International Releases: October 10th, 2014 (Wide) (Venezuela)
November 14th, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)
April 10th, 2015 (Wide) (Germany)
Video Release: February 10th, 2015 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for rude humor including some reckless behavior and language.
(Rating bulletin 2320, 4/30/2014)
Running Time: 81 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Intertitle, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Curses, Job Interview, Non-Chronological, Performing Arts, Relationships Gone Wrong, Wish Granters
Social setting: Prom, Publishing Industry, Theater
Subgenre: Family Comedy
Target audience: Family Movie
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 21 Laps Entertainment, Jim Henson Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

11-year-old Alexander experiences the most terrible and horrible day of his young life—a day that begins with gum stuck in his hair, followed by one calamity after another. But when Alexander tells his upbeat family about the misadventures of his disastrous day, he finds little sympathy and begins to wonder if bad things only happen to him. He soon learns that he’s not alone when his mom, dad, brother and sister all find themselves living through their own terrible, horrible, no good, very bad day. Anyone who says there is no such thing as a bad day just hasn’t had one.
